epics.title: Foundations & Infrastructure
epics.summary: Project structure, database schema, Docker Compose infrastructure, and test harness.

stories.title: As the team, I want a Clean Architecture project structure so backend development follows SOLID principles.
acceptance_criteria: Given the solution file, When built, Then Domain, Application, Infrastructure, and API projects compile with no circular dependencies.
Given the Domain project, When inspected, Then it has zero external NuGet dependencies.
tasks: Create solution file and 4 layer projects (Domain, Application, Infrastructure, Api)
Configure project references (Api -> Infrastructure -> Application -> Domain)
Add MediatR, EF Core, FluentValidation, ErrorOr, Serilog NuGet packages

stories.title: As the team, I want the database schema and EF Core migrations so all persistence is versioned.
acceptance_criteria: Given a clean PostgreSQL database, When migrations run, Then Orders, OutboxMessages, and TraceEvents tables exist with correct columns and indexes.
Given the Orders table, When inspected, Then Id is UUID PK, Status is varchar(50), and timestamps are present.
tasks: Define Order entity with OrderStatus enum

stories.title: As the deployer, I want Docker Compose infrastructure so the entire system spins up with one command.
acceptance_criteria: Given docker-compose.yml, When docker-compose up -d runs, Then PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Redis containers are healthy.
Given RabbitMQ container, When accessed, Then management plugin is available on port 15672.
tasks: Create docker-compose.yml with PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ (management plugin), Redis

stories.title: As the team, I want a Testcontainers integration test harness so tests run against real PostgreSQL.
acceptance_criteria: Given the test project, When dotnet test runs, Then a PostgreSQL container spins up, migrations apply, and tests execute.
Given a test class, When it uses WebApplicationFactory, Then the API uses the Testcontainers database.
tasks: Set up WebApplicationFactory with test DbContext
Verify migrations run in test container

epics.title: Core API & Outbox Pattern
epics.summary: Application layer, outbox pattern, Correlation ID middleware, API versioning, Swagger, validation, and core REST endpoints.

stories.title: As the system, I want CQRS commands and queries so order creation and retrieval are decoupled.
acceptance_criteria: Given CreateOrderCommand, When handled, Then a new Order is saved with Pending status.
Given GetOrderQuery, When handled, Then the order is returned by Id.

stories.title: As the system, I want the outbox pattern so order creation and event publishing are atomic.
acceptance_criteria: Given CreateOrderCommand, When handled, Then Order and OutboxMessage are saved in the same database transaction.
Given a failed save, When the transaction rolls back, Then neither Order nor OutboxMessage exists.
tasks: Add OutboxMessage creation to CreateOrderCommand handler
Ensure shared transaction across Order and OutboxMessage

stories.title: As the system, I want Correlation ID middleware and structured logging so every request is traceable.
acceptance_criteria: Given an incoming request without X-Correlation-Id header, When processed, Then a new GUID is generated and appears in every log line.
Given an incoming request with X-Correlation-Id header, When processed, Then the existing ID is propagated to logs.
tasks: Verify CorrelationId appears in console logs

stories.title: As a recruiter, I want versioned REST endpoints and Swagger UI so I can explore the API interactively.
acceptance_criteria: Given the API, When accessed at /swagger, Then interactive documentation for all v1 endpoints is available.
Given POST /v1/orders, When called with valid body, Then it returns 201 Created with the order.
Given GET /v1/orders/{id}, When called, Then it returns 200 OK or 404 NotFound.
tasks: Configure API versioning with URL path prefix (/v1/)
Implement GET /v1/orders endpoint with cursor pagination

stories.title: As the system, I want input validation and error handling so invalid requests fail gracefully.
acceptance_criteria: Given POST /v1/orders with empty customerName, When validated, Then 400 Bad Request with field-level errors.
Given GET /v1/orders/{id} with non-existent id, When queried, Then 404 NotFound with ErrorOr format.
tasks: Write unit tests for validation rules

epics.title: RabbitMQ Integration & Worker
epics.summary: RabbitMQ topology, outbox dispatcher, Worker service, idempotency, and dead-letter queues.

stories.title: As the system, I want explicit RabbitMQ topology so messaging is transparent and interview-explainable.
acceptance_criteria: Given the system starts, When RabbitMQ is inspected, Then traceflow.order-events, traceflow.order-status-updates, and traceflow.dlx exchanges exist with correct bindings.
Given a message fails 3 times, When retried, Then it routes to traceflow.dead-letter queue.
tasks: Configure direct exchanges for order-events and order-status-updates
Configure dead-letter exchange and dead-letter queue
Set queue properties: durable, manual ack, dead-letter routing

stories.title: As the system, I want an outbox dispatcher so pending messages are reliably published to RabbitMQ.
acceptance_criteria: Given pending OutboxMessages, When the dispatcher polls, Then up to 10 are fetched and published sequentially.
Given a publish failure, When the error occurs, Then the message Error field is updated and remaining messages continue processing.
tasks: Implement batch read (max 10) and sequential publish
Implement per-message failure handling with Error column update
Implement ProcessedAt update after successful publish

stories.title: As the system, I want a Worker service that consumes RabbitMQ messages so process separation is demonstrated.
acceptance_criteria: Given the Worker starts, When it connects to RabbitMQ, Then it begins consuming from traceflow.worker.order-events queue.
Given a message is received, When processed, Then it is manually acked.
tasks: Add CorrelationId extraction from AMQP headers to logging scope

stories.title: As the system, I want the Worker to simulate status progression and publish updates so the full async flow works.
acceptance_criteria: Given Worker receives OrderCreated, When processing, Then it publishes OrderStatusChanged events at realistic intervals (3s/7s/4s/6s).
Given status changes, When published, Then the same CorrelationId is propagated in AMQP headers.
tasks: Implement status progression logic with Task.Delay
Propagate CorrelationId in published message headers

stories.title: As the system, I want retry policy and dead-letter queues so failed messages don't block the pipeline.
acceptance_criteria: Given a message processing fails, When retried, Then it is redelivered up to 3 times.
Given 3 retries exhaust, When the final nack occurs, Then the message routes to the dead-letter queue.
tasks: Implement basicNack with requeue=false on final failure
Verify dead-letter queue receives failed messages

stories.title: As the system, I want idempotency so duplicate messages are gracefully ignored.
acceptance_criteria: Given a duplicate OrderCreated message, When consumed, Then the Worker logs a warning and acks without reprocessing.
Given the idempotency set, When inspected, Then it contains processed OrderIds.
tasks: Document known limitation (in-memory only, lost on restart)

epics.title: Real-time Updates, Caching & Health
epics.summary: API background consumer, SignalR Hub, Redis caching with explicit invalidation, trace events, and health checks.

stories.title: As the API, I want a background consumer for status updates so the Worker and API communicate through the queue.
acceptance_criteria: Given the API starts, When it connects to RabbitMQ, Then it consumes from traceflow.api.order-status-updates with prefetchCount=1.
Given a status update message, When received, Then the Order row is updated in PostgreSQL.
tasks: Create API background consumer hosted service
Implement Order status update in PostgreSQL

stories.title: As the system, I want a SignalR Hub so the API can push real-time updates to connected clients.
acceptance_criteria: Given a client connects, When it calls JoinOrderGroup(orderId), Then it receives OrderStatusUpdated events for that order.
Given a status update is processed, When pushed via IHubContext, Then only clients in that order's group receive it.
tasks: Wire background consumer to push via IHubContext

stories.title: As the API, I want Redis caching so order lookups are fast.
acceptance_criteria: Given GET /v1/orders/{id}, When first called, Then the order is cached in Redis with 30s TTL.
Given a cached order, When requested again within 30s, Then the response comes from Redis (no DB query).
tasks: Add Redis caching to GetOrderQuery handler

stories.title: As the system, I want explicit cache invalidation so clients never see stale data.
acceptance_criteria: Given a status update is received, When processed, Then the cached order entry is removed from Redis.
Given cache invalidation occurs, When a client requests the order, Then the fresh data is fetched from PostgreSQL and re-cached.
tasks: Implement cache invalidation in background consumer
Verify invalidation triggers on every status update

stories.title: As the frontend, I want a trace endpoint so the full distributed journey is visible.
acceptance_criteria: Given GET /v1/orders/{id}/trace, When called, Then all TraceEvents for that order are returned in chronological order.
Given the trace response, When inspected, Then it includes CorrelationId, Step, Service, Status, and Timestamp for each event.
tasks: Ensure TraceEvents are written by all services (API, Dispatcher, Worker)

stories.title: As an operator, I want a health endpoint so I know all dependencies are healthy.
acceptance_criteria: Given GET /health, When all dependencies are up, Then 200 OK with Healthy status for PostgreSQL, Redis, and RabbitMQ.
Given Redis is down, When GET /health is called, Then 503 Unhealthy with Redis marked as Unhealthy.
tasks: Implement health checks for PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ

epics.title: React Frontend
epics.summary: Next.js frontend with FSD/Atomic Design, SignalR, status timeline, trace visualizer, dark mode, and idempotency demo.

stories.title: As the team, I want a Next.js project with FSD and Atomic Design so the frontend is well-organized.
acceptance_criteria: Given the project structure, When inspected, Then it follows FSD layers (app, pages, widgets, features, entities, shared).
Given shared/ui, When inspected, Then it contains atoms, molecules, organisms, and templates folders.
tasks: Create Next.js project with App Router
Set up Atomic Design within shared/ui/
Add base shadcn components (Button, Card, Input, Badge) to shared/ui/atoms/

stories.title: As a user, I want dark mode support so the UI feels modern.
acceptance_criteria: Given the app loads, When the system preference is dark, Then the UI renders in dark mode.
Given a manual toggle, When clicked, Then the theme switches and persists.
tasks: Add theme provider and toggle component
Apply dark: variants to existing components

stories.title: As a user, I want to create an order so the demo flow begins.
acceptance_criteria: Given the home page, When I enter customerName and click create, Then an order is created and the CorrelationId is captured.
Given a successful creation, When the response arrives, Then the page navigates to ?order={id} and shows the order detail.
tasks: Implement navigation with orderId query param

stories.title: As a user, I want a status timeline so I can see the order's progress visually.
acceptance_criteria: Given the order detail page, When loaded, Then the current status is shown with a visual timeline.
Given a live status update, When received via SignalR, Then the timeline animates to the new status.
tasks: Implement status steps visualization (Pending → Paid → Preparing → Shipped → Delivered)
Style with Tailwind (including dark mode variants)

stories.title: As a user, I want SignalR real-time updates so the status changes without refreshing.
acceptance_criteria: Given the order detail page, When loaded, Then it connects to SignalR and calls JoinOrderGroup(orderId).
Given the connection drops, When it happens, Then auto-reconnect with exponential backoff begins.
Given the order reaches Delivered, When it happens, Then SignalR disconnects automatically.

stories.title: As a recruiter, I want a trace visualizer so I can see the distributed system in action.
acceptance_criteria: Given the trace panel, When loaded, Then it shows a chronological list of events with service names, timestamps, and CorrelationId.
Given live events, When they arrive, Then the trace updates with animated connecting lines between services.
tasks: Create event timeline with connecting lines
Add color-coding by service (API, Dispatcher, Worker)
Add animated dots for live updates

stories.title: As a user, I want to share an order link so the demo is shareable.
acceptance_criteria: Given a URL with ?order={id}, When loaded, Then the order detail and trace are fetched and displayed.
Given a page refresh, When it happens, Then the same order state is restored without data loss.
tasks: Handle page load with orderId from query params
Fetch order and trace on initial load

stories.title: As a demo user, I want to trigger a duplicate message so idempotency is visually demonstrated.
acceptance_criteria: Given the trace panel, When the Trigger Duplicate button is clicked, Then a duplicate OrderCreated event is sent to RabbitMQ.
Given the duplicate is processed, When the Worker ignores it, Then the trace shows a warning icon with 'Duplicate message detected — ignored by Worker'.
tasks: Create Trigger Duplicate button (dev/demo mode only)
Implement API endpoint or direct RabbitMQ publish for duplicate
Update trace visualizer to show duplicate warning

epics.title: Dockerization & VPS Deployment
epics.summary: Dockerfiles for all services, Nginx reverse proxy, Cloudflare SSL, and production deployment.

stories.title: As the deployer, I want Dockerfiles for all services so they run consistently.
acceptance_criteria: Given docker-compose up, When it runs, Then API, Worker, and Web containers build and start successfully.
Given the API container, When it starts, Then EF migrations run automatically.

stories.title: As the deployer, I want Nginx as a reverse proxy so traffic routes correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Given a request to api.getsynka.com, When it arrives, Then Nginx proxies it to the API container on port 5000.
Given a request to app.getsynka.com, When it arrives, Then Nginx proxies it to the Web container on port 3000.
tasks: Create Nginx config with server blocks for app.getsynka.com and api.getsynka.com

stories.title: As the deployer, I want Cloudflare + SSL so the site is secure.
acceptance_criteria: Given app.getsynka.com, When accessed, Then it serves HTTPS via Cloudflare.
Given api.getsynka.com, When accessed, Then it serves HTTPS via Cloudflare.
tasks: Configure Cloudflare DNS A records for app and api subdomains

stories.title: As the deployer, I want deployment documentation so I can deploy manually.
acceptance_criteria: Given the deployment docs, When followed, Then the entire system is running on the VPS within 30 minutes.
Given the .env file, When configured, Then no secrets are hardcoded in any file.
tasks: Write VPS setup guide (Docker, Docker Compose, Nginx)
Document troubleshooting (logs, health checks, RabbitMQ management)
